paper_title: The Pliocene Model Intercomparison Project (PlioMIP) Phase 2: scientific objectives and experimental design

methods.method_name: Model Output Data Handling and Formatting
methods.description: Procedures for formatting, processing, and storing model simulation output data according to PlioMIP Phase 2 guidelines, including specific requirements for core experiments designated as CMIP6/PMIP simulations and general constraints for all experiments.

steps.description: If the PlioMIP Phase 2 core experiment is adopted as a CMIP6 simulation, model data for this experiment must use the Climate Model Output Rewriter (CMOR) format.
steps.searchable_summary: Format climate model output data using CMOR library for core experiments
inputs: Raw model output data from PlioMIP Phase 2 core experiments

steps.description: If the PlioMIP Phase 2 core experiment is adopted as a CMIP6 simulation, model data must be stored on an ESGF node.
steps.searchable_summary: Store CMOR-formatted model data on ESGF node for core experiments

steps.description: PlioMIP Phase 2 has modified the established variable list outlined by the third phase of the PMIP project. The list of required variables can be found on the PlioMIP Phase 2 website.
steps.searchable_summary: Ensure model output variables match the required list from the PlioMIP Phase 2 website
inputs: PlioMIP Phase 2 required variable list (from website)
outputs: Validated set of model output variables

steps.description: All model outputs will be submitted initially to a data repository at the University of Leeds (including the PlioMIP Phase 2 core experiment, which may have data replicated in CMOR format on an ESGF node).
steps.searchable_summary: Submit all model output data to the University of Leeds data repository
inputs: Model output data files (various formats, including potentially CMOR)
outputs: Data stored in University of Leeds repository

steps.description: The data files have to be in the (now widely used) NetCDF binary file format.
steps.searchable_summary: Convert model output data files to NetCDF format
inputs: Model output data files (potentially in various internal formats)

steps.description: Data files must conform to the CF (Climate and Forecast) metadata convention.
steps.searchable_summary: Add or validate CF metadata in NetCDF files

steps.description: There must be only one output variable per file.
steps.searchable_summary: Split multi-variable NetCDF files into single-variable files
outputs: NetCDF files, each containing a single variable

steps.description: For the data that are a function of longitude and latitude, only regular grids (grids representable as a Cartesian product of longitude and latitude axes) are allowed.
steps.searchable_summary: Ensure or regrid spatial data to regular grids

steps.description: The file names have to follow the PMIP2 file name convention and be unique.
steps.searchable_summary: Rename data files according to PMIP2 convention and ensure uniqueness
outputs: Renamed data files with unique PMIP2-compliant names
